title: Day 8: AI Content Systems — One Idea, Every Format

steps.title: Welcome to Week 2 — Real Work
steps.body:
Week 1 was about learning the tools. Week 2 is about producing real value.

Today you'll build a content system — a pipeline that takes one core idea and turns it into a blog post, social media posts, email newsletter, and landing page copy — all with consistent voice and messaging.

This is what separates an AI dabbler from an AI operator: systems that produce at scale.

The content pipeline:

One core idea → Blog post (1,500 words) → 5 social posts → 1 email newsletter → Landing page → Schedule & publish

Each step uses AI to adapt, not rewrite. The core message stays consistent — only the format changes.

The operator's skill: crafting the one core idea that feeds the entire system.

steps.question: What is the key principle of an AI-powered content system?
options: Generate completely different content for each platform
Start with one core idea, then adapt it for each format while keeping consistent messaging
Use AI to replace all human writers entirely
Post the same text everywhere with no changes
feedback: Exactly. A content system starts with one strong idea and adapts it for each platform. The message is consistent, the format varies.
Not quite. The magic is consistency across formats — one idea expressed optimally for each channel, not random content or verbatim copies.

steps.title: From One Idea to Full Content System
steps.prompt:
Core Idea: 'Small businesses waste 10 hours/week on admin tasks they could automate with AI.'

Pipeline Output:

1. Blog Post (1,500 words): 'The 10-Hour Drain: How Small Businesses Waste Time on Admin (And How AI Fixes It)' — Comprehensive guide with stats, examples, step-by-step solutions

2. LinkedIn Post: 'I asked 50 small business owners where they waste the most time. 90% said admin. The fix? A $20/month AI automation.' — Personal insight, short, engaging

3. Twitter/X Thread (5 tweets): '1/5 Most small business owners don't need to hire more people. They need to use AI better. Here's how.' — Scannable, shareable

4. Email Newsletter: 'This Week's Insight: The Admin Time Trap' — Value-driven with practical tips

5. Landing Page: 'Cut Admin Time by 70% With AI' — Benefit-focused, CTA-driven

One idea. Five formats. Consistent message.

steps.title: Build Your Content Pipeline
steps.instruction:
Pick a topic you know well — your industry, a hobby, a business problem you've solved.

Write it as a single core idea sentence. Then list 3-5 formats you'd adapt it for:

Core Idea: [One sentence capturing the key insight]

Formats:
1. [Format] — [What angle you'd take]
2. [Format] — [What angle you'd take]
3. [Format] — [What angle you'd take]

Be specific about the angles — the format changes, but the core idea stays.
steps.placeholder:
Core Idea: Real estate agents who use AI for property descriptions save 5 hours/week and close 20% more leads.

Formats:
1. Blog post — Step-by-step guide with before/after examples
2. Instagram Reel — 60-second transformation story
3. Email to agents — Quick wins they can implement today
4. LinkedIn article — Industry insight with data
5. Landing page — 'AI for Real Estate Agents' with case study

questions.prompt: Look at your current content output (emails, social, documents). What's one topic you've written about multiple times? How could a content pipeline save you time?
questions.placeholder: A recurring topic and how a system would help...

questions.prompt: What's the single most valuable piece of content you could create right now — not the biggest, but the one that would create the most leverage?
questions.placeholder: The core idea and why it matters...

steps.title: Day 8 Complete!
steps.body:
You've designed your first content pipeline. One idea, multiple formats, consistent message.

Tomorrow: You'll become an AI-powered research assistant — producing deep research briefs in minutes instead of hours.
